title: Recast as time-to-next-pit with censoring, then derive next-lap risk
summary: This task is naturally an event-time problem.
rationale: This task is naturally an event-time problem. XGBoost’s AFT survival objective supports censored labels, which matches rows near race end or rows where the next pit is not observed within the remaining laps. That is a cleaner inductive bias than plain binary classification on every row.
implementation_hint: For each training row, compute laps until the driver’s next pit; use exact intervals when observed and right-censor rows that reach race end without another pit. Train an AFT model or discrete hazard sidecar, then convert predicted survival into `P(pit in next lap)` via `S(t)-S(t+1)` or a 1/2/3-lap hazard head. Blend this score with the base classifier.
expected_effect: Could improve ranking around ambiguous pit-window rows by using full time-to-event structure instead of a one-step label only.
risk: Label construction is subtle around current-lap pits, red-flag-like anomalies, and final stints. If censoring logic is wrong, performance will drop despite a sound objective.
